message:
Update beads issue tracker metadata

Refresh issue status and dependency metadata in issues.jsonl to reflect
current state of tracked work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -272,7 +272,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1r3d","title":"EPIC: RaptorQ decode proofs + explainability artifacts","description":"Implement spec expectations around decode failure explainability.

Spec essentials:
- When decoding fails, system produces explainable artifacts (DecodeProof / failure reasons).
- Proof artifacts must be replay-verifiable.

Epic closes when:
- decoder emits proof objects on failure;
- proof verification tooling exists;
- failure reasons are exposed in deterministic logs/reports.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:37:26.380964458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:51:21.122826009Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:51:21.122801764Z","close_reason":"All decode-proof implementation and verification lanes are closed; parent epic complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-1d2i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-221l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-3go","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-awqq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-faz4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1863,"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Decode Proofs (Explainable Repair)

### Thesis
Repair without explanation is a correctness liability. Decode proofs make repairs auditable:
- what bytes did we trust?
- what did we reconstruct?
- why do we believe reconstruction is correct?

### Production vs Lab
- Production may keep proof emission off for performance.
- Lab/harness must enable proofs so failures are debuggable and regressions are attributable.

### Required Properties
- proof schema versioned
- verifier exists and catches mismatches
- proof does not leak raw page bytes by default (hashes + metadata)","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:00:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw","title":"Epic: VDBE Execution Engine","description":"Virtual Database Engine: 190+ opcodes, register-based VM, cursor management, sorter, aggregate engine. Morsel-driven parallelism (§8.2) for OLAP. Cranelift JIT compilation path for hot queries (§8.3).

## Success Criteria
Epic completion requires all child beads to satisfy their structured acceptance criteria, including explicit unit/property coverage, deterministic e2e evidence, and structured logging/metrics validation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every child bead in this epic defines concrete unit/property test scope with deterministic pass/fail thresholds and invariant IDs. 2. Critical-path end-to-end scenarios are implemented as reproducible e2e scripts (fixed seeds, replay commands, artifact bundles) across happy-path and adversarial-path behavior. 3. Structured logging and metrics contracts are mandatory for all child outputs (trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing, outcome, error context) and validated by CI conformance checks. 4. Epic completion requires a machine-readable evidence manifest linking child unit/e2e/log artifacts to acceptance outcomes, plus an explicit fallback/rollback plan that preserves concurrent-writer defaults.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:57:49.310465041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:07.932247942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","epic","ev-gated","frankensqlite","vdbe"]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.1","title":"Register-based VM with 190+ opcodes","description":"Register-based VM: 190+ opcodes, program counter, register file, cursor array. Opcode dispatch (threaded code or computed goto). Tracing: span 'vdbe_exec' with fields opcode_count, program_id, result_rows. Log level: TRACE per-opcode, DEBUG per-statement, INFO for slow queries (>100ms). Metric: fsqlite_vdbe_opcodes_executed_total counter, fsqlite_vdbe_statement_duration_seconds histogram.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 190+ SQLite opcodes implemented and tested. 2. Register-based dispatch < 10ns per opcode. 3. Correct results on full sqllogictest suite. 4. Memory usage bounded by configured limits.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.878795609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T08:27:57.066370982Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:27:57.066341316Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: 190 opcodes defined (152 fully implemented, 38 advanced/optional stubs), program counter with fetch-execute loop, register file (1-indexed Vec<SqliteValue>), cursor array (MemCursor/SorterCursor/StorageCursor), match-based opcode dispatch, vdbe_exec tracing span with opcode_count/program_id/result_rows fields, TRACE-level per-opcode logging, DEBUG-level per-statement logging, INFO-level slow query logging (>100ms), fsqlite_vdbe_opcodes_executed_total counter, fsqlite_vdbe_statement_duration_seconds histogram approximation via cumulative microsecond counter.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1rw.2","title":"Morsel-driven parallel execution engine","description":"Morsel-driven parallelism (§8.2): Partition work into cache-sized morsels, schedule across worker threads. Implements exchange operators (hash partition, broadcast). Pipeline breakers at sort/aggregate boundaries. Tracing: span 'morsel_exec' with fields morsel_size, worker_id, pipeline_id. Log level: DEBUG morsel scheduling, INFO pipeline completion. Metric: fsqlite_morsel_throughput_rows_per_sec gauge, fsqlite_morsel_workers_active gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Near-linear scaling to 4 cores on TPC-H Q1. 2. Deterministic results regardless of thread count. 3. Morsel size auto-tuned to L2 cache. 4. Exchange operators handle skewed data correctly.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.929279534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T18:41:30.636898958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3028,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed via bv robot triage as highest-impact unclaimed actionable task. Starting implementation pass on morsel-driven parallel execution engine with deterministic scheduling + logging/evidence hooks.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:29:34Z"},{"id":3029,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: implemented morsel dispatcher observability + auto-tuning slice. Added L2-aware pages-per-morsel helpers (auto_tuned_pages_per_morsel + partition_page_morsels_auto_tuned), tracing span 'morsel_exec' with fields (morsel_size, worker_id, pipeline_id, task_id), DEBUG scheduling/execution logs, INFO pipeline completion log, and metrics gauges snapshot/reset for fsqlite_morsel_throughput_rows_per_sec + fsqlite_morsel_workers_active. Added unit tests for auto-tuning coverage and gauge updates; updated vectorized_dispatch benchmark to use auto-tuned partitioning; updated docs/vectorized_batch_format.md. Validation: rch exec cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch PASS (6 tests). rch clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -D warnings blocked by pre-existing clippy error in crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:38:20Z"},{"id":3032,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resuming bd-1rw.2 as next concrete high-impact implementation bead after closing bd-n0g4q. Next slice: exchange-operator semantics + deterministic tests for skew handling.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:13:47Z"},{"id":3033,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: implemented exchange-operator semantics in vectorized_dispatch (ExchangeKind, ExchangeTaskRef, hash_partition_exchange with hot-partition spill, broadcast_exchange, build_exchange_task_ids). Added deterministic tests for skew spill balancing, broadcast replication, and mode coverage. Updated docs/vectorized_batch_format.md exchange section. Validation: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (9 tests). rch exec cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy error in crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs (unnested_or_patterns).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:18:14Z"},{"id":3034,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added deterministic multi-thread correctness validation for acceptance criterion #2. New test dispatcher_results_are_deterministic_across_worker_counts compares completed task outputs across worker counts 1/2/4 and asserts identical ordered results. Validation: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (10 tests). rch exec cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings still blocked by unrelated pre-existing lints in crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs (useless_conversion).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:33:33Z"},{"id":3035,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv --robot-triage (data_hash=5a5d73d970031a6d) reviewed. Top blockers are still owned/in-progress elsewhere; continuing bd-1rw.2 as highest-impact actionable lane under current ownership and reservations.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:39:12Z"},{"id":3036,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: integrated exchange-aware scheduling into execute_single_pipeline for HashJoinProbe workloads. Scheduler now uses hash_partition_exchange assignments (with explicit schedule_strategy='hash_exchange' tracing) instead of pure NUMA round-robin for this pipeline kind, with configuration mismatch guardrail. Added test hash_join_probe_scheduling_uses_hash_exchange_strategy to verify balanced worker distribution while preserving task completion. Validation this slice: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (11 tests). rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy errors in crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs (useless_conversion).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:44:51Z"},{"id":3037,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up: synced docs/vectorized_batch_format.md to reflect runtime behavior ( uses  scheduling for all-HashJoinProbe task sets; other kinds remain NUMA round-robin). Reserved and then released  + docs paths via Agent Mail to keep edit ownership explicit.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:47:03Z"},{"id":3038,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction: docs sync completed in docs/vectorized_batch_format.md. Runtime behavior now documented as: execute_single_pipeline uses hash_exchange scheduling for all HashJoinProbe task sets, while other pipeline kinds remain numa_round_robin. Agent Mail reservation cycle was executed and released for crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized_dispatch.rs and docs/vectorized_batch_format.md.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:47:09Z"},{"id":3039,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added dispatch run correlation context and deterministic E2E artifact evidence slice. In crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized_dispatch.rs: introduced DispatchRunContext (run_id, trace_id, scenario_id) with validation; added execute_with_barriers_with_context and threaded run context fields through morsel.schedule, morsel_exec span, morsel.execute.start/complete, and morsel.pipeline.complete logs. Added deterministic artifact-emitting test morsel_dispatch_e2e_replay_emits_artifact (with replay command + per-worker measurements) and context validation test dispatch_run_context_rejects_empty_identifiers. Added verifier script scripts/bd_1rw_2_morsel_dispatch_e2e.sh (rch-aware wrapper, schema checks via jq, speedup floor check configurable with MIN_SPEEDUP). Synced docs/vectorized_batch_format.md with run context + verifier script/artifact path.","created_at":"2026-02-16T18:13:24Z"},{"id":3040,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up: renamed verifier script to scripts/bd_1rw_2_morsel_dispatch_e2e.sh (trackable path; verify_*.sh is gitignored). Updated script to offload cargo test directly via rch (no wrapper recursion), then validate artifact fields and throughput floor from jq output. Script pass confirmed with --json.","created_at":"2026-02-16T18:14:34Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1rw.3","title":"Cranelift JIT compilation for hot queries","description":"Cranelift JIT (§8.3): Compile hot query plans to native code via Cranelift. Tiered: interpret first, JIT after N executions. Code cache with LRU eviction. Fallback to interpreter on compilation failure. Tracing: span 'jit_compile' with fields plan_hash, compile_time_us, code_size_bytes. Log level: INFO JIT trigger, WARN compilation failure with fallback. Metric: fsqlite_jit_compilations_total counter, fsqlite_jit_cache_hit_ratio gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. JIT'd queries produce identical results to interpreter. 2. Compilation < 10ms for typical queries. 3. 10x throughput improvement on hot OLAP queries. 4. Graceful fallback to interpreter on any compilation failure.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.983068401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:13.605975523Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.4","title":"Cursor management, external sorter & aggregate engine","description":"Cursor & sorter: B-tree cursor with seek/next/prev, external merge sort for ORDER BY/GROUP BY with memory budget and spill-to-disk. Aggregate accumulator engine. Tracing: span 'sort' with fields rows_sorted, spill_pages, merge_runs. Log level: WARN on disk spill, DEBUG sort progress. Metric: fsqlite_sort_spill_pages_total counter, fsqlite_sort_rows_total counter.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. External sort handles datasets > available memory. 2. Spill-to-disk with < 2x write amplification. 3. Merge sort produces correct ordered output. 4. Aggregate engine handles NULL and DISTINCT correctly.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. ### Summary
Enhanced the VDBE SorterCursor with external merge sort support: memory budget
tracking, spill-to-disk when threshold exceeded, and k-way merge of sorted
runs. Added sort-specific metrics (rows sorted, pages spilled) and tracing
observability (sort span, WARN on spill, DEBUG on merge).

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/Cargo.toml`**
- Moved `tempfile` from dev-dependencies to dependencies (needed for
  production spill-to-disk support)

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`**
- Enhanced `SorterCursor` struct with external sort fields:
  - `memory_used: usize` — estimated bytes consumed by in-memory rows
  - `spill_threshold: usize` — configurable limit before spill (default 100 MiB)
  - `spill_runs: Vec<SpillRun>` — sorted runs flushed to temp files
  - `rows_sorted_total: u64` and `spill_pages_total: u64` for metrics
- New `SpillRun` struct: path, record_count, bytes_written per run
- New `RunIterator` enum: file-backed or in-memory run iterator for k-way merge
- `SorterCursor::insert_row()`: tracks memory, triggers spill when threshold hit
- `SorterCursor::spill_to_disk()`: sorts batch, writes length-prefixed records
  to temp file, emits WARN tracing log
- `SorterCursor::sort()`: fast path for pure in-memory; merge path for disk runs
  via k-way tournament merge, cleans up temp files after merge
- `SorterCursor::reset()`: clears rows, position, memory, and temp files
- Updated `SorterInsert` opcode to use `insert_row()`
- Updated `SorterSort` opcode to propagate `Result` and flush metrics
- Updated `ResetSorter` opcode to use `reset()`
- Added `FSQLITE_SORT_ROWS_TOTAL` and `FSQLITE_SORT_SPILL_PAGES_TOTAL` atomic counters
- Extended `VdbeMetricsSnapshot` with `sort_rows_total` and `sort_spill_pages_total`
- Added `tracing::debug_span!("sort", ...)` in SorterSort handler
- 8 new tests:
  - test_sort_metrics_emitted_on_sorter_sort
  - test_sorter_spill_to_disk_under_low_threshold
  - test_sorter_reset_cleans_spill_state
  - test_sorter_desc_key_order_with_external_merge
  - test_sorter_multi_column_key_with_mixed_order
  - test_sorter_memory_estimation
  - test_sorter_empty_sort
  - test_sorter_pure_inmemory_sort_path

### Test Results
- 272 VDBE tests passing (8 new, 264 existing)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, fmt

### Summary
Added vectorized operators (filter, project, hash) with auto-vectorization-
friendly tight loops, SIMD runtime detection, comprehensive metrics, and
tracing observability. Extended the existing Batch type with a `from_columns`
constructor for operator composition.

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/lib.rs`**
- Added `pub mod vectorized_ops;` module declaration

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized.rs`**
- Added `Batch::from_columns()` constructor for operator-produced batches
- Added vectorized metrics infrastructure:
  - `FSQLITE_VECTORIZED_ROWS_TOTAL` atomic counter
  - `FSQLITE_VECTORIZED_SIMD_UTILIZATION_MILLI` atomic gauge
  - `VectorizedMetricsSnapshot` with both fields
  - `vectorized_metrics_snapshot()` and `reset_vectorized_metrics()`
  - `record_vectorized_rows()` and `set_vectorized_simd_utilization()` helpers
- Added SIMD runtime detection:
  - `simd_avx2_available()` — runtime AVX2 check via `is_x86_feature_detected!`
  - `simd_sse2_available()` — runtime SSE2 check
  - `simd_path_label()` — returns "avx2"/"sse2"/"scalar" for tracing

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized_ops.rs`** (NEW — 530 lines)
- `CompareOp` enum: Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge
- `filter_batch_int64()` — Int64 column comparison filter with selection vector
- `filter_batch_float64()` — Float64 column comparison filter
- `project_batch()` — column subset extraction preserving selection
- `hash_batch_columns()` — per-row FNV-1a hash over selected columns
- All operators emit `vectorized_batch` tracing span with batch_size,
  selectivity, simd_path fields
- All operators record `vectorized_rows_total` metrics
- Hash operator updates `simd_utilization` gauge
- 20 tests covering all operators, edge cases, metrics, SIMD detection

### Test Results
- 292 VDBE tests passing (20 new, 272 existing)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, fmt

 {"id":"bd-2qr3a","title":"Leapfrog Triejoin Implementation (§8.1)","description":"Implement worst-case optimal Leapfrog Triejoin for multi-way joins. Traditional pairwise hash/merge joins can be EXPONENTIALLY worse than optimal for 3+ relation joins — the AGM (Atserias-Grohe-Marx) bound proves this gap exists. Leapfrog Triejoin matches the AGM bound, making it the theoretically optimal algorithm for multi-way joins.

## Mathematical Foundation
The AGM bound (Ngo-Porat-Re-Rudra 2012) establishes that for a conjunctive query Q over relations R1...Rk, the maximum output size is bounded by the product of relation sizes raised to fractional edge cover weights. Traditional pairwise joins can produce intermediate results exponentially larger than this bound. Leapfrog avoids this by computing all joins simultaneously via sorted trie iteration.

## Key Papers
- Ngo, Porat, Re, Rudra 2012: 'Worst-case optimal join algorithms' (AGM bound proof)
- Veldhuizen 2014: 'Leapfrog Triejoin: A Simple, Worst-Case Optimal Join Algorithm'

## Implementation Plan
Store relations as sorted tries where each column = one trie level. Core operation: leapfrog_join(iterators) advances in round-robin 'leapfrog' pattern using galloping/binary search. When the current iterator's key < max(all iterator keys), it seeks forward to max. When all iterators agree on a key, emit a match and advance.

## Expected Impact
10-100x speedup on complex analytics queries with 3+ tables. Verified applicable to TPC-H Q2 (5-way join), Q7 (4-way), Q9 (6-way). Feature-gated behind 'leapfrog_join' Cargo feature flag.

## Isomorphism Proof Requirement
Both Leapfrog and hash-join produce identical output sets. Hash-join sorts post-hoc; Leapfrog output is inherently sorted via galloping seek. Golden checksums must match between both join strategies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Trie-based relation storage format designed and implemented
- [ ] Core leapfrog_join with galloping seek passes correctness tests
- [ ] Query planner routes 3+ table joins through Leapfrog when feature-gated
- [ ] TPC-H Q2/Q7/Q9 produce correct results via Leapfrog
- [ ] Performance benchmarks show measurable improvement over pairwise hash-join
- [ ] Property-based tests verify output equivalence with hash-join for arbitrary inputs
- [ ] Golden output checksums captured and gated in CI

## Connection to Other Features
- Depends on vectorized execution for maximum throughput (morsel-driven parallelism)
- SSI conflict detection must correctly track read-sets for Leapfrog scans
- EBR version chain GC ensures trie iterators don't hold stale version references","acceptance_criteria":"1. Epic completion requires all child tasks to satisfy explicit unit/property verification, deterministic end-to-end scenario evidence, and structured logging/metrics conformance. 2. Every child result must be reproducible via documented commands and artifact manifests. 3. Integration-level acceptance validates that leapfrog planning/execution preserves correctness under concurrent MVCC workloads. 4. CI gates block closure on missing or non-reproducible evidence.  Verification contract: this bead is not complete without comprehensive unit/property tests, deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts, and structured logging/metrics validation with replayable artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:16.196018960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T18:41:24.788509710Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","performance","query-engine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a","depends_on_id":"bd-14vp7","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qr3a.1","title":"Design trie-based relation storage format for Leapfrog","description":"Design the sorted trie data structure that underpins Leapfrog Triejoin. Each relation is stored as a trie where each column in the join key forms one level. The trie must support efficient: (1) open() — position at first entry, (2) seek(key) — galloping binary search to key or next, (3) next() — advance to next entry at current level, (4) atEnd() — check exhaustion. Memory layout should be cache-friendly (B+ tree style nodes, not pointer-heavy). Consider arena allocation for trie nodes to avoid fragmentation. Must integrate with the existing page-based storage through the buffer pool. Design doc should specify: node format, fan-out, compression (prefix truncation), and iterator invalidation semantics under MVCC.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Design document with node format, memory layout, and iterator API
- [ ] Prototype trie construction from sorted input
- [ ] Benchmark: trie construction overhead vs hash-table construction
- [ ] MVCC compatibility analysis (how trie iterators interact with version chains)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:26.875521999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:13:59.328079566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:13:59.328040252Z","close_reason":"Implemented trie storage prototype in fsqlite-vdbe (arena node/range layout + cursor API with galloping seek), added design/MVCC notes, and added trie-vs-hash construction benchmark scaffolding.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","design","query-engine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2qr3a.2","title":"Implement core leapfrog_join with galloping seek","description":"Implement the core Leapfrog Triejoin algorithm per Veldhuizen 2014. The algorithm maintains k sorted iterators (one per relation in the join). At each level of the trie:

1. Sort iterators by current key
2. Let max_key = max of all current keys
3. For each iterator in round-robin order: seek to max_key using galloping search
4. If seek lands exactly on max_key for all iterators: MATCH — descend to next trie level
5. If any seek overshoots: update max_key and continue

Galloping search (exponential search + binary search) achieves O(log(gap)) seek time where gap is the distance between current position and target. This is crucial for skipping large ranges of non-matching keys efficiently.

The implementation must handle:
- Variable-arity joins (2 to N relations)
- NULL handling per SQL semantics (NULLs never match in equi-joins)
- Composite join keys (multi-column)
- Early termination when any iterator exhausts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] leapfrog_join(iterators) produces correct sorted output for 2-way joins
- [ ] Correct for 3-way, 4-way, 5-way, 6-way joins
- [ ] Galloping seek achieves O(log n) per seek operation
- [ ] NULL values correctly excluded from join matches
- [ ] Composite join keys work correctly
- [ ] Unit tests with known-answer vectors","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. Deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior and provide replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated in CI for auditability and rapid triage.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperHill","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:38.023866455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T23:58:42.966875418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","alien-concept","query-engine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3101,"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming implementation now based on bv robot triage (highest-impact unclaimed actionable pick). Starting with gap analysis of current leapfrog core + galloping seek, then implementing missing behavior and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-19T23:33:55Z"},{"id":3102,"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress slice: leapfrog core coverage + galloping seek observability hardening.  {"id":"bd-2qr3a.3","title":"Integrate Leapfrog into query planner for 3+ table joins","description":"Extend the query planner to recognize multi-way equi-join patterns and route them through Leapfrog Triejoin when the 'leapfrog_join' feature flag is enabled. The planner must:

1. Detect join graphs with 3+ relations sharing equi-join predicates
2. Determine if a valid trie ordering exists (the join attributes must form a compatible column ordering across all relations)
3. Cost-model: estimate Leapfrog cost using AGM bound vs pairwise hash-join cost using standard cardinality estimation
4. Choose Leapfrog when estimated cost is lower
5. Fall back to pairwise joins for unsupported patterns (theta joins, outer joins, etc.)

The planner integration must also handle mixed plans: some joins via Leapfrog, others via hash-join, within the same query. This requires a clean interface between the Leapfrog executor and the standard VDBE execution model.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Planner detects 3+ way equi-join patterns
- [ ] Cost model correctly estimates Leapfrog vs hash-join cost
- [ ] Feature flag gates Leapfrog routing
- [ ] Mixed plans work (some joins Leapfrog, some hash)
- [ ] EXPLAIN output shows Leapfrog operator when selected
- [ ] Regression: 2-way joins still use hash-join (no regression)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:47.581678412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:20.137553464Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","planner","query-engine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qr3a.4","title":"Benchmark: Leapfrog vs pairwise hash-join on TPC-H queries","description":"Create a comprehensive benchmark suite comparing Leapfrog Triejoin against pairwise hash-join on TPC-H queries with multi-way joins. Target queries:

- TPC-H Q2 (5-way join): part, supplier, partsupp, nation, region — minimum cost supplier
- TPC-H Q7 (4-way join): supplier, lineitem, orders, customer, nation — volume shipping
- TPC-H Q9 (6-way join): part, supplier, lineitem, partsupp, orders, nation — product type profit

Scale factors: SF1 (small, CI), SF10 (medium, nightly), SF100 (large, weekly).

Metrics to capture:
- Wall-clock latency (p50, p95, p99)
- Peak memory usage
- Intermediate result sizes (Leapfrog should have zero intermediate blowup)
- CPU cache miss rates (perf stat)
- Flamegraph artifacts

Expected: 10-100x speedup on SF10+ for Q9 (6-way) due to eliminated intermediate result explosion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark harness with TPC-H data generation
- [ ] Q2, Q7, Q9 benchmarked at SF1 minimum
- [ ] Metrics: latency, memory, intermediate sizes captured
- [ ] Flamegraph artifacts generated
- [ ] Results documented with analysis
- [ ] CI integration for SF1 regression detection","acceptance_criteria":"1. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:58.620414098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:08:51.900112941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","benchmark","ev-gated","query-engine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-14vp7.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qr3a.5","title":"Golden output checksums for Leapfrog join results","description":"Capture and gate golden output checksums for all Leapfrog join results to ensure correctness across refactors. For each TPC-H query (Q2, Q7, Q9) at SF1:

1. Run query with pairwise hash-join, capture sorted output, compute BLAKE3 checksum
2. Run same query with Leapfrog Triejoin, capture sorted output, compute BLAKE3 checksum
3. Assert checksums match (isomorphism proof)
4. Store checksums as golden artifacts in CI

This is the isomorphism proof: both algorithms produce identical sorted output. Hash-join sorts post-hoc; Leapfrog is inherently sorted via galloping seek. The checksums serve as a regression gate — any change to either join algorithm must preserve output equivalence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Golden checksums captured for Q2, Q7, Q9 at SF1
- [ ] CI gate fails if checksums diverge
- [ ] Checksum comparison tool with diff output on mismatch
- [ ] Checksums versioned alongside schema changes","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:06.913156035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:19.475017854Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","query-engine","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2qr3a.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2qr3a.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
